Every action must include purpose, expected signal, and failure condition.
thinking_steps: Start with constraints: define authorized scope, target assumptions, and failure-safe boundaries.
Convert raw command outputs into evidence statements before selecting any exploit path.
Generate at least two competing hypotheses and rank them by evidence strength.
Choose the next command only if it can disambiguate hypotheses or gather missing proof.
Re-score confidence after each evidence update and pivot quickly when a path is disproven.
Explicitly track uncertainty and unresolved questions to avoid overfitting to known room narratives.

decision_points: Escalate only when evidence supports exploitability.

confidence_guidance.high: Multiple independent artifacts confirm the same hypothesis.
confidence_guidance.medium: Some evidence exists but alternate explanations remain viable.
confidence_guidance.low: Insufficient or contradictory evidence; gather more telemetry.
